contentList.content:
Drink to probe into the minds of those around you. You will be able to read the thoughts of certain creatures while speaking with them.

Fragments of memory and perception smatter this liquid - a crude constellation of thought.

Drink to slow your rate of descent during falls and gain Immunity to Falling damage.

Tossing this vial from hand to hand, it seems to glide, suspended in motion, before coming to rest gently in your palm.

Drink to gain the ability to Fly.

This potion is so light, you feel that if you tossed it high, it might float like a lilypad on the pond of the air.

Drink this to triple your Jump distance.

Lupperdiddle Swires, Gnomish Adventurer, could reportedly jump twenty feet in the air. He was about the size of a bantam hen.

Cures Blinded, Silenced, Paralysed, and Poisoned creatures.

This muddy sludge glitters with otherworldly magic.

Drink to fall into a 2-turn slumber. Unless interrupted, you gain the benefits of a Long Rest.

One of the few ways for elves to experience sleep. Other methods include taking narcotics and being hit very hard with a chair.

Drink to fall into a 2-turn slumber. Unless interrupted, you gain the benefits of a Short Rest and regain all 1st and 2nd Level spell slots. If you are awoken before the duration expires, the effects will not apply.

The motion of the shimmering liquid going back and forth in its vial is somnambulant - you know drinking it will bring the comfort of a warm, enveloping blanket.

Gain Resistance to Acid damage.

Despite its reputation as a chalky and unappetising brew, a pleasing effervescence makes this potion easy to drink.

Gain the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts until your next Long Rest.

A heady aroma of hay, musk, and manure greets your nose upon opening the bottle.

Neutralises the effects of all poisons.

This concoction appears rather chewable, thick as it is with herbs and roots.

Permanently increase an ability score by 2.

A reward received from biting Araj Oblodra (or after stealing it). Vampirism has sometimes been called The Dark Gift, or the Dark Kiss, perhaps as an allusion to their forbidden relationship to sunshine. It does provide gifts in abundance; such is the case with this potion.

Increase a creature's strength Strength by 4. Stacks with elixirs that do not change Strength.

Angling this solution just so refracts the colours of sunrise within.

You gain Resistance to Fire damage, and can no longer Burn. This elixir can be thrown at a target to apply its effects.

Pleasantly cool to the touch. A layer of frost coats the inside of the bottle.

Transform into a cloud of mist. You become Resistant to non-magical damage, gain Advantage on Constitution, Dexterity and Strength saves, and become tiny. You can't attack, cast spells, or talk.

Consuming this potion feels more like swallowing vapour than liquid.

Heals some health immediately and some on the next turn, and removes Burning.

A draught for curing minor cuts and bruises.

Heals some health immediately and some on the next turn, and removes Burning.

A draught for curing moderate injuries.

Heals some health immediately and some on the next turn, and removes Burning.

A draught for curing serious injuries.

Heals some health immediately and some on the next turn, and removes Burning.

A draught for healing life-threatening injuries.

Gain 10 temporary hit points and become Blessed.

Flasks filled with this potion are often passed around by soldiers at first light on the dawn of battle.

Increase your Strength by 2. Stacks with elixirs that do not change Strength.

A sliver of a hill giant's fingernail is suspended in this potion, and the foul stench renders it unpleasant to swallow.

Drink to turn Invisible.

You'd think this bottle was empty but for the sound of sloshing liquid from within.

Gain Resistance to all damage.

Thick as molten iron with a taste to match. Coats you from your throat to your fingertips.

You gain Resistance to Lightning damage and can no longer be Shocked, Electrocuted, Jolted, or otherwise affected by lightning. This elixir can be thrown at a target to apply its effects.

This potion causes a tingling sensation in the extremities when consumed.

Gain Resistance to Necrotic damage. This elixir can be thrown at a target to apply its effects.

Smells of fresh herbs and berries. Some say flowers placed within will bloom indefinitely.

You gain Resistance to Poison damage and can no longer be Poisoned. This elixir can be thrown at a target to apply its effects.

Popular among politicians and adventurers alike.

Gain Resistance to Psychic damage. This elixir can be thrown at a target to apply its effects.

This potion, reminiscent of whiskey in taste but not effect, is known to cause temporary ringing in the ears.

Gain an extra action, a +2 bonus to Armour Class, Advantage on Dexterity Saving Throws, and double your movement speed.

This solution ripples and splashes of its own accord, almost like it's trying to escape the bottle.

Drink to gain a +5 bonus to Initiative. In addition, you can't be Surprised. .

An acrid, coppery aroma wafts from this liquid, clearing the senses and honing awareness.

Drink to increase the effectiveness of your spells. You will gain 1 Arcane Acuity.

Derived from the eye of a mage-hunting Nothic, this elixir waxes from within with a piercing light.

Drink to enter a bloodlust. Once per turn when you kill a foe, you gain 5 temporary hit points and an additional action. .

Those willing to immerse themselves in savagery often reap a blood-red reward.

Drink to gain Advantage on Concentration saves, and against being Charmed. Magic can't put you to sleep. .

A spellcaster who cannot maintain their spells is about as useful as a chocolate kettle.

The number you need to roll to land a Critical Hit is reduced by 1. .

The syrupy liquid languidly swirling inside this bottle somehow reminds you of a predator lying in wait.

Drink to gain the ability to see in the dark up to 12 foot. .

Enfolded in darkness, monsters and tucked-away treasures hide, glimpsed only by the naturally gifted, and, of course, whosoever imbibes this brackish elixir.

Drink to increase your size. Weapons deal an additional 1d4 damage. You gain Advantage on Strength Checks and Saving Throws. .

Instantly upon inhaling this elixir, your abdomen seems to swell, and the muscles in your back feel like sheets of hammer-beaten steel.

Your movement speed can't be reduced by Difficult Terrain or spells. You can't be Paralysed or Restrained. .

Though textured like half-frozen honey, this liquid flows as effortlessly as mercury.

Gain an additional Level 1 spell slot. .

A small bottle, filled to the brim with arcane energy.

Gain an additional Level 2 spell slot. .

From hedge wizards to high priests, these elixirs are coveted by spellcasters of any ilk.

Gain an additional Level 3 spell slot. .

Even through the thick glass, the arcane energy contained within this bottle makes your fingers tingle.

Gain an additional Level 4 spell slot. .

It is said that the glimmer in this elixir is the reflection of light on the Weave itself.

Drink this elixir to spot Invisible creatures. .

Beneath its vial of glass the liquid floods with colour, goes translucent, and becomes colourful once more - a pretty fluctuation.

Gain Psionic Weakening

Stimulates the pineal gland in the brain, unlocking malevolent power.

Drink to increase your Armour Class to 16. .

Before your eyes, the elixir firms into a tough, striated twig, then it liquefies once more.

You gain Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion Checks, Resistance to Acid damage, and can use Acid Arrow once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

You have Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion Checks, Resistance to Fire damage, and can use Fireball once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

You have Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion Checks, Resistance to Cold damage, and can use Ice Storm once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

You have Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion Checks, Resistance to Lightning damage, and can use Lightning Bolt once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

You have Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion Checks, Resistance to Poison damage, and can use Ray of Sickness once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

Dwarven blood lets you add your Proficiency Bonus to Saving Throws until you succeed on 3 of them or take a Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

Noble elven blood grants you an additional 10m movement speed, Darkvision, and you can't be Charmed until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

A mote of githyanki psionics allows you to cast Misty Step, Invigorate Leap, and Blur each only once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

You are Proficient in Sleight of Hand Checks and can cast Knock until your next Long Rest thanks to the crafty wiles of the Forgotten Folk.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

Upon consumption, you and surrounding allies regain hit points equal to your Constitution score.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

The luck of the smallfolk grants you Advantage on Skill Checks until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

When you are Downed before your next Long Rest, you instead drop to 1 hit point thanks to the relentless will roiling in your veins.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

Mundane pluckiness grants you Proficiency in all skills until the next Long Rest due to the wily resourcefulness now bubbling within it.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

You can call on diabolic arcana to cast Thaumaturgy, Hellish Rebuke, Burning Hands, and Flame Blade each only once until your next Long Rest.

One of Blood Vendor Araj Obladra's elixirs, distilled from a specific sanguine quality, and with properties aligned to an equally specific race.

Bathe a nearby area in light.

Now and again, a small ball of light flickers in the dark liquid, like the reflection of some alien yet sweet star on the darkest ocean.

Lob this vaporous decoction to Befuddle nearby foes.

Inhaling the wrong spores can dissolve even the sharpest intellect into fizzing confusion.

Explodes in a cloud of invigorating energy that Hastens creatures within.

An alchemical concoction practically buzzing with potential energy.

Explodes into a virulent cloud that can inflict Noxious Fumes upon creatures within.

A rank, roiling stench emanates from this alchemical concoction.

Difficult terrain - Movement Speed is halved. Can Enweb creatures. Prevents Falling damage. Flammable.

This tangle of webs pulses with expectant magic, eager to unfurl at a moment's notice.

Deals 1d4 Fire damage per turn.

Shapes swirl and glow behind the glass, liquid embers folding into and around each other.

Blind a target. If it is a steel watcher, it is Stunned, and suffers a -1d6 penalty to Attack Rolls.

Gyroscopic whispers emanate from the device - gnomish machinery speaks with a metal tongue.

Force a hag that has consumed a child to disgorge it instantly.

In the land of Prismeer, where fair Zybilna reigns, they had their share of hag trouble, oh yes. They had it in spades.

Hags have a special predilection for children and the power inherent to innocence - disrupting one in the consumption of that power could be cataclysmic for the hag.

Coat your weapon in an oil that strips targets that are Resistant to Fire damage of their Resistance.

Targets that are Immune to Fire damage are unaffected.

'It was then that Swires tossed the oil. It splattered the cambion, and all at once its skin of brilliant carmine dulled to dark, a red like cheap wine, and the fiend vented this sound of anguish I almost pitied, for its very affinity for fire was now undone.'

On hit, a coated weapon covers its target in oil for 2 turns. Oil-covered targets immolate upon taking fire damage, dealing 3d6,Fire Damage to everyone and everything nearby.

'Look, it's perfectly simple. You just coat the person in the oil, like so. Then you strike a match, like s- oh bugger.' - Bufort Iggles, The Unfortunate

Coat your weapon to receive a +2 bonus to Attack Rolls with the oiled weapon.

This slick oil helps your weapon slip through the tiniest of cracks in an enemy's defences.

Coat your weapon in oil. Targets failing a DC 11 Charisma Saving Throw become Baned for 2 turns.

The cloying scent of this oil makes one feel foolish, ill-prepared, not quite worthy.

Coat your weapon in oil. It gains a +1 bonus to Damage and Attack Rolls, and overcomes Resistance to non-magical damage.

The flecks of shimmering crystal suspended within this murky oil make it hum with arcane potential.

Coat your active weapon in oil. When you hit a foe, it inflicts Encrusted with Frost for 2 turns.

Coils of frost spiral against the vial's glass - in hushed environments, you can even hear it crackling.

Coat your weapon in oil: it receives a -1 penalty to damage, but targets failing a DC 11 Constitution save, suffer Disadvantage on Strength Checks and saves. They also receive a -1d4 penalty to melee damage.

Gazing through the glass bottle, everything seems shrunken and contracted - almost gaunt.

Coat your weapon in oil. Its targets receive a -3 penalty to Spell Attack Rolls and Spell Save DC, and Disadvantage on Saving Throws for maintaining Concentration for 2 turns.

Symbols for the eight schools of magic fluctuate in this oil - one by one appearing and dissolving in the oil, as if devoured.

Coat your active weapon with poison. Targets must succeed a DC 15 Constitution Saving Throw or become Blinded and Poisoned.

This vial contains a fine powder that resembles smoke when shaken - or tossed into an enemy's eyes.

Coat your active weapon with poison. Targets must succeed a DC 11 Saving Throw or become Poisoned.

Even when closed, a choking acrid smell emanates from this bottle.

Coat your active weapon with a toxin. Targets of this weapon take 1d4,Poison damage at the end of their next turn, unless they succeed a DC 11 Constitution Saving Throw.

The emerald hue of this toxin is vivid and baleful as a sunrise over a swamp.

Coat your active weapon with a toxin. Targets of this weapon take 1d8,Poison damage at the end of their next turn, unless they succeed a DC 15 Constitution Saving Throw.

Shifting mercurially within its vial, the toxin holds a promise of swift and delectable death.

Coat your active weapon with a toxin. Targets of this weapon take 1d6,Poison damage at the end of their next turn, unless they succeed a DC 13 Constitution Saving Throw.

From political assassinations to marital dysfunction, poison's got you covered.

Coat your active weapon with a toxin. Targets of this weapon take 1d10,Poison damage at the end of their next turn, unless they succeed a DC 19 Constitution Saving Throw.

If the poison is potent enough, even the smallest pinprick can fugue the brain and turn the capillaries to shrivelled tubes.

Coat your active weapon with poison. Targets must succeed a DC 13 Constitution Saving Throw or become Poisoned and fall Asleep.

Many drow cultivate poisons with religious fervour. After all, Lolth, their dread arachnid Goddess, is pleased by only the deadliest of concoctions.

Coat your active weapon in poison. Targets must succeed a DC 17 Constitution Saving Throw or become Poisoned and take 1d6,Poison damage at the end of each of its turns.

A truly despicable poison devised from an agglutination of corpse roses by the experimental mind of Thisobald Thorm.

Toss a cluster of 3 bombs that can knock nearby creatures Prone. Each bomb will explode in a Fiendish Flameblast when triggered.

This improvised grenade was handcrafted by skirmisher-devils to be used in infernal combat.

Coat your active weapon with poison. Targets must succeed a DC 13 Constitution Saving Throw or become Paralysed and Poisoned.

The base for this sticky membranous poison was scraped from the carapace of a carrion crawler.
